title: Unit test suite — node:test, full coverage of pure logic, GIA hook
description: Add a complete unit test suite using Node's built-in node:test runner (zero new dependencies). Cover all pure logic modules. Also create a GIA hook that runs the full test suite after every task so regressions are caught at merge time.

features: Add 'test' script to package.json: 'node --test test/**/*.test.js'
Create test/ directory with the following test files:
FILE: test/config.test.js — tests for src/config.js
validateUrl: valid http/https URLs pass; non-URLs, empty strings, ftp:// fail with correct error messages
validateDate: valid ISO dates (YYYY-MM-DD, full ISO 8601) pass; garbage strings fail
validateConfig: valid minimal config passes; missing name fails; invalid link fields fail; multiple errors are all collected (not short-circuited); empty string name fails
findConfig: finds links.yaml in cwd; finds links.yaml in parent dir; stops at .git boundary and throws; throws at filesystem root
readConfig/writeConfig round-trip: write a config object, read it back, assert deep equal
FILE: test/utils.test.js — tests for src/utils.js (isLinkActive)
No dates: always returns true
Only scheduled_from in the past: returns true
Only scheduled_from in the future: returns false
Only scheduled_until in the future: returns true
Only scheduled_until in the past: returns false
Both dates, now within window: returns true
Both dates, now before window: returns false
Both dates, now after window: returns false
Bare date (YYYY-MM-DD) for scheduled_until: the entire day is active (test at 23:59:59 of that day)
Null/undefined link: returns false
FILE: test/generator.test.js — tests for src/generator.js
filterScheduled: filters inactive links correctly, passes active ones through, handles empty array
extractAccentColor: extracts --accent-color from CSS string; returns fallback on missing

generatePage: output contains <!DOCTYPE html>; contains config.name (escaped); contains config.bio; contains all active link labels; does not contain inactive scheduled link labels; contains Powered by KNTIC Links footer; contains <link rel='icon'>
generatePage with XSS-candidate name/bio/label: output must have HTML-escaped values, no raw < > & ' " in output
FILE: test/secret.test.js — tests for src/secret.js
secretPath: returns correct absolute path
readSecret: returns key from valid file; returns null for missing file; returns null for empty file; trims whitespace
FILE: test/themes.test.js — tests for src/themes/loader.js
listThemes: returns array containing all 5 expected theme names
loadTheme: loads minimal-dark successfully; throws descriptive error for unknown theme
Use node:test and node:assert/strict throughout. Use import { test, describe } from 'node:test'.
For findConfig tests that need real filesystem: use node:os tmpdir() to create temp directories, write/delete files in test setup/teardown using test hooks (before/after).
GIA HOOK: create .kntic/hooks/gia/specific/001-unit-tests.sh
Shebang: #!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
Change to the repo root (dirname of the script, up three levels from hooks/gia/specific/)
Run: npm test
Exit 0 on success, non-zero on any test failure (set -e handles this)
chmod 755 the file
Add a brief comment at the top: '# GIA hook: run unit tests after every task'

notes: node:test is built into Node 18+ — zero new dependencies. Test files use ES module syntax (package.json has type:module). For filesystem tests (findConfig), always clean up temp dirs in after() hooks even if the test fails. The GIA hook runs npm test from the repo root — ensure the script resolves the repo root correctly using: REPO_ROOT=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../.." && pwd). Generator tests do not need to write to disk — generatePage() returns a string, just assert on the string content.
